response.justification: Bogdan has broad full-stack experience and tangentially relevant geospatial work (GISCollective, Green Map GIS), but the core requirement of modern C++ or Rust expertise is essentially unmet — his C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis project. His primary commercial stack is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript, and his geospatial experience is hobbyist or NGO-scale rather than commercial navigation/routing pipelines. The distributed systems and low-latency work at Sociomantic (DLang) shows relevant systems instincts, but it is insufficient to offset the fundamental language and domain gaps for a routing tiling pipeline role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS experience through GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, caching, millions of requests/sec)
Strong TDD and software craftsmanship culture with broad cross-stack exposure
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust in any commercial context; last C/C++ work was 2014–2015 and is incidental to the role
Geospatial experience is entirely hobby/NGO-level — no commercial routing, navigation tiles, or map data pipeline work
Primary expertise (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) is largely irrelevant to a systems-level C++ routing pipeline
